content.full_text:
Re:
Farn Polrucal Pna.crrcps CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 8O'7 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 9, 2000
Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California Street, Ste. 1410
San Francisco, CA 94108
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-00-075
Dear Mr. Bagatelos:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act').r Because you have not identified a particular client on whose behalf you are
requesting this advice, we are treating your letter as a request for informal assistance.
(Re gulation t 823 9 (b)(zXB ). )'
QUESTION
When is a group of citizens required to file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) in
connection with the qualification of a measure?
CONCLUSION
The Statement of Organizationmust be filed within 10 days once the issue becomes a
"measure" under the Act and the group has received contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a
calendar year to support or oppose its qualification or passage.
I Government Code sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear attitIe2, sections 18109-
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity conferred by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83 I I 4; Regulation I 8239(c)(3).)


File No. I-00-075
Page2
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates and committees to file periodic reports disclosing
contributions received and expenditures made in connection with state and local elections.
"Committee" includes any person or group of persons that receives contributions totaling $1,000
or more in a calendar year for the purpose of supporting or opposing candidates or for the
qualification, pussage, or defeat of a ballot measure. (Sections 82013(a) and 82015; Regulation
1821s.)
The term "measure" is defined in section 82043 to mean:
"... any constitutional amendment or other proposition which is
submitted to a popular vote at an election by action of a legislative
body, or which is submitted or is intended to be submitted to a
popular vote at an election by initiative, referendum or recall
procedures whether or not it qualifies for the ballot."
Inits Fontarea opinion, (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 25, the Commission held that a proposal
submiued to the voters by a legislative body does not become a "measure" under the Act until the
legislative body orders it placed on the ballot. The Commission further held that persons raising
or spending funds to support or oppose the measwe have no campaign disclosure obligations
until that time. An initiative, referendum, or recall procedure becomes a "measure" under the Act
when the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions to qualiff the measure for the ballot.
Once a proposal becomes a measure, all contributions received arid expenditures made
must be reported, including those received and made before the campaign reporting requirements
were triggercd. (Angus Advice Letter, No. A-97-173.) The terms "contribution,,and
"expenditure" are defined as payments made for "political purposes." (Sections 82015 and
82025.) "Political purpose" means that the payment was made for the pu{pose of influencing or
attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a
candidate or candidates, or the qualification or passage of any meosure. (Regulations
1 82 1 s(a)(l ) and t8225(ax1 ).)
The first frling required for a group collecting frrnds for political purposes is the
committee Statement of Organization (Form 410), due within l0 days of receiving contributions
totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. (Section 84101.) Reading this provision in
conjunction with the Fontana opinion, however, the l0-day filing period for a group that raises
$1,000 in contributions prior to an issue becoming a measure would not begin to run until the
issue is placed on the ballot or, in the case of an initiative, referendum or recall procedure, until
the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions. If the issue never becomes a measure, no
filing obligations will be incurred.


File No. I-00-075
Page 3
If you need further assistance or have questions concerning this letter, please contact me
at(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
C^u-uJAt*t
By
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

written advice. (Section 83 I I 4; Regulation I 8239(c)(3).)
